name: Bookkeeping
tagline: Automate categorization, reconciliation, and recurring entries with workflows that keep client books on your machine.
heroTitle: An AI bookkeeper that runs on a schedule.
heroSubtitle: Categorize transactions, reconcile accounts, and chase receipts with workflows that run on your own machine and on a schedule. Client books never leave your device unless you say so, and every run is governed, logged, and auditable.
intro: Bookkeeping is repetitive, deadline-driven, and full of sensitive client data that doesn't belong in a public chatbot. Ryu lets you build workflows that categorize, reconcile, and follow up on a schedule, connect into your accounting tools, and log every run, so the routine work runs itself while a human reviews the result.

highlights.title: Runs on a schedule
highlights.description: Chain agents and tools into workflows with triggers, so weekly categorization and reconciliation runs without you starting it.

highlights.title: Connects to your ledger
highlights.description: Agents authenticate into QuickBooks, Xero, banks, and spreadsheets to read transactions and post drafts for your review.

highlights.title: Books stay on your machine
highlights.description: Run a local Gemma 4 on install, or route to a cloud model with PII and DLP redaction on every request.

highlights.title: Auditable by default
highlights.description: Every run is logged in the Gateway audit trail, so you can show exactly what a workflow touched and when.

useCases.title: Transaction categorization
useCases.description: Classify incoming transactions against each client's chart of accounts, learning their rules and flagging the unclear ones.

useCases.title: Recurring reconciliation
useCases.description: Run a scheduled workflow that matches bank and card feeds to the ledger and reports the unmatched items for review.

useCases.title: Receipt and invoice chasing
useCases.description: Detect transactions missing a receipt and draft the follow-up requests, then track which clients still owe documents.

useCases.title: Multi-client batch processing
useCases.description: Process the same routine across a book of clients on a schedule, with a per-client summary of what needs attention.

useCases.title: Recurring journal entries
useCases.description: Draft standard recurring entries like depreciation and prepaids each period for a bookkeeper to approve.

useCases.title: Exception reporting
useCases.description: Surface duplicates, miscategorizations, and unusual amounts across the books, each tied back to the entry it found.

examplePrompts: Set up a weekly workflow that categorizes new transactions in Xero against each client's chart of accounts.
Reconcile this bank feed to the ledger and list every transaction that didn't match.
Find every transaction this month missing a receipt and draft the requests to the client.
Draft this period's recurring depreciation and prepaid entries for me to approve.
Scan the books for duplicate or miscategorized entries and show me where each one is.

faq.q: Is client bookkeeping data safe with Ryu?
faq.a: Yes. Ryu is local-first: you can run a model entirely on your machine with no telemetry. When you route to a cloud model, the Gateway redacts PII and sensitive data on egress and logs the run, so client books stay governed end to end.

faq.q: Can workflows run on their own schedule?
faq.a: Yes. Workflows chain agents and tools and can run on schedules and triggers, so recurring categorization and reconciliation happen without you starting them. Each run is logged so you can see what it did.

faq.q: Will it post entries without me?
faq.a: No. Ryu drafts categorizations, reconciliations, and entries for a bookkeeper to review and approve. The audit trail records every run, so the review and any cleanup are quick and defensible.

ctaTitle: Give your practice an AI bookkeeper.
ctaSubtitle: Categorize, reconcile, and follow up on a schedule, with client books kept private and every run on the record.
ctaNote: Scheduled workflows · Local-first · Full audit trail
